name: Aixola Reservoir Loop from Ermua
name_original: Presa de Aixola – circular desde Ermua
description: This difficult 10.7-mile touring cycling route from Ermua offers challenging climbs, varied terrain, and scenic views of the Embalse de Aixo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Ermua offers diverse landscapes and an extensive network of trails in the western Duranguesado region of the Basque Country. The area is characterized by valleys carved between steep hillsides, with the town protected by the Urko and Ureta mountains. A significant natural feature is the Aixola Reservoir, which is integrated into several cycling routes. The region's varied natural scenery provides a scenic backdrop for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Ermua The most popular touring cycling route is Aixola Reservoir Loop from Ermua, a 10.7 miles (17.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This route features the scenic Aixola Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Trabakua Pass – Aixola Reservoir loop from Ermua, a difficult 24.6 miles (39.6 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and passes through the Trabakua Pass. Local touring cyclists also love the Ermua – Areitio Climb loop from Ermua, a 21.3 miles (34.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with a notable climb, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Ermua is defined by rugged valleys, steep hillsides, and the picturesque Aixola Reservoir. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Ermua are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 270 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ermua's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Ermua?
A: Touring cycling around Ermua offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll encounter rugged valleys and steep hillsides, with the town protected by the Urko and Ureta mountains. Many routes feature a mix of paved and unpaved sections, with significant elevation gains, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40372936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trabakua Pass – Aixola Reservoir loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt;. The region's varied natural scenery provides a scenic backdrop for your rides.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Ermua?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Ermua are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular options include the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085201445/aixola-reservoir-loop-from-ermua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aixola Reservoir Loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.7 miles (17.3 km) long, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15004082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermua – Areitio Climb loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt;, covering 21.3 miles (34.2 km) with notable climbs.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling in Ermua?
A: The region around Ermua is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see striking valleys, steep hillsides, and the picturesque Aixola Reservoir, which is integrated into several cycling routes. The area is also within reach of major natural attractions like the Urdaibai natural reserve and Urkiola Natural Park, offering a scenic backdrop for your rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31780842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besaide Monument – Aixola Reservoir loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt; is one route that highlights these features.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easier touring cycling routes in Ermua?
A: While many routes in Ermua are rated moderate to difficult due to elevation gains, there are options that might be more accessible. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38267638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermua Town Hall – Ermua loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 24.6 miles (39.6 km) route with less elevation gain compared to others, making it a good choice for those seeking a less strenuous ride. It's always recommended to check the route details for distance and elevation to match your family's fitness level.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Ermua?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ermua are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenge of the climbs, and the scenic beauty of areas like the Aixola Reservoir. The variety of terrain, from paved sections to more technical paths, also contributes to a rewarding experience for many.

Q: Are there routes with significant climbs for advanced cyclists?
A: Yes, Ermua is known for its challenging climbs that attract advanced cyclists. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40372936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trabakua Pass – Aixola Reservoir loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gain, including passing through the Trabakua Pass. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15004082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermua – Areitio Climb loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt; also includes a notable climb, offering a demanding experience for those seeking a workout.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I explore near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, Ermua and its surroundings offer several historical points of interest. Within the town, you can find the Renaissance Church of Santiago Apóstol and the Baroque Valdespina Palace. Nearby, you might consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/609786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Zenarruza&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4056489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Miguel de Arretxinaga&lt;/a&gt;, which are listed as attractions in the wider region.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ermua?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around Ermua, catering to various skill levels and preferences. These routes range from moderate to difficult, offering diverse landscapes and challenges for touring cyclists.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Ermua?
A: The Basque Country generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from spring through autumn. While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the diverse landscapes with mountains and reservoirs suggest that milder temperatures would be ideal. Avoiding the peak heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter winter months would likely enhance your touring cycling experience.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes directly on the routes are not detailed, Ermua itself has a vibrant community with amenities. Given the popularity of cycling in the region, it's common to find establishments in towns and villages along or near the routes where you can stop for refreshments. Planning your route to pass through these areas can ensure convenient breaks.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes near Ermua?
A: Yes, some routes around Ermua offer longer distances for touring cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31780842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besaide Monument – Aixola Reservoir loop from Ermua&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 29.4 miles (47.3 km) long, providing a more extended ride through the region's varied terrain and natural features.
tips: Located in the Duranguesado region, the town of Ermua is known for its rich history and its beautiful natural landscape. Here you can enjoy the local gastronomy. The town's name may come from the Basque term Emuas, which means border area or terrain, due to its location on the dividing line between the provinces of Vizcaya and Guipúzcoa.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the Presa de Aixola – circular desde Ermua route is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness. It involves significant elevation gain (460 meters) over its 17.3-kilometer length, with a mix of paved and unpaved sections. Some parts may even require dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Presa de Aixola circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain. Approximately 13 kilometers are paved roads, while about 2.96 kilometers consist of paths, which can be unpaved or challenging. Cyclists should be prepared for considerable elevation changes throughout the loop.

faqs.question: How do I get to the starting point in Ermua?
faqs.answer: The starting point in Ermua is accessible by public transport, making it convenient for touring cyclists. You can plan your journey to Ermua using local transport services.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Presa de Aixola circular route?
faqs.answer: While the route starts in Ermua, which is accessible by public transport, specific parking information for cyclists is not detailed. It is advisable to check local parking options in Ermua before your visit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing the Presa de Aixola – circular desde Ermua route is approximately 1 hour and 23 minutes (5013 seconds) for an average touring cyclist. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in a beautiful natural landscape with dense vegetation. Key highlights include the Aixola Reservoir, which is a significant water supply, and the impressive Presa de Aixola (Aixola Dam), standing over 50 meters high. You'll also pass by the Ermua Town Hall at the start/end and the Alto de Karabieta, offering picturesque views of the Duranguesado region.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Presa de Aixola route?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through areas with lush landscapes, suggesting that spring and autumn might offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, but be prepared for potentially warmer weather.

faqs.question: Is the Presa de Aixola – circular desde Ermua route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify whether this particular cycling route is dog-friendly. As it involves significant cycling on varied terrain, it might not be ideal for all dogs. If you plan to bring a dog, it's recommended to check local regulations or consider alternative, more pedestrian-focused routes.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of any specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Presa de Aixola – circular desde Ermua route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to rest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily through natural landscapes. While the starting point in Ermua will have amenities, specific cafes or rest stops directly along the 17.3 km cycling loop are not detailed. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of 'Elgeta - Aixola urtegia' (33% of the route), 'Bizkaiko bira' (26%), and 'E08 Bizkar ibilbidea: Eibar - Elgeta - Udala' (18%). You'll also find smaller segments overlapping with 'Eibarko Itzulia', 'Intxorta - Asentzio', and others.
